snp_knowledge.condition: MTHFR C677T - Reduced enzyme activity affecting folate metabolism
snp_knowledge.description: The T allele reduces MTHFR enzyme activity by ~35% (heterozygous) or ~70% (homozygous), impairing conversion of folate to methylfolate.

avoid.reason: Synthetic form may accumulate and block folate receptors
Depletes B vitamins and impairs methylation

interventions.action: Increase leafy greens, liver, legumes for natural folate

snp_knowledge.description: This variant reduces BH4 production, affecting dopamine, serotonin, and nitric oxide synthesis.

interventions.action: Regular exercise to boost natural BH4 production

snp_knowledge.condition: MTR A2756G - Altered B12-dependent methionine synthase
snp_knowledge.description: Affects methionine synthase activity, impacting homocysteine to methionine conversion. May increase B12 requirements.

interventions.action: B12-rich foods: organ meats, shellfish, nutritional yeast

snp_knowledge.condition: MTRR A66G - Reduced methionine synthase reductase
snp_knowledge.description: Reduces ability to regenerate active B12, compounding MTR issues. Higher B12 requirements.
Upregulated CBS drains homocysteine toward sulfur pathway, potentially depleting methionine and SAMe.

supplements.name: Low-dose B6
supplements.rationale: CBS cofactor - too much speeds pathway

interventions.action: Monitor sulfur food tolerance: eggs, garlic, cruciferous

snp_knowledge.description: Reduces alternative homocysteine recycling pathway via betaine. Important if MTHFR compromised.

interventions.action: Beets, spinach, quinoa are natural betaine sources

snp_knowledge.description: Met/Met (AA) has 3-4x slower COMT. Benefits: better focus. Risks: anxiety, estrogen dominance.

snp_knowledge.condition: MAOA R297R - Altered monoamine oxidase A
snp_knowledge.description: Affects serotonin, dopamine, norepinephrine breakdown. Low activity linked to aggression, high activity to depression risk.

snp_knowledge.condition: APOE e4 - Increased Alzheimer's and cardiovascular risk
snp_knowledge.description: APOE e4 is the strongest genetic risk factor for late-onset Alzheimer's. One copy 3x risk, two copies 12x.

supplements.name: Omega-3 (DHA)
supplements.rationale: Critical - e4 carriers have impaired DHA transport

snp_knowledge.condition: Factor V Leiden - Increased clotting risk
snp_knowledge.description: Most common inherited clotting disorder. Increases DVT and PE risk significantly.

interventions.action: Compression socks for flights, walk hourly

snp_knowledge.description: Increases prothrombin levels by 30%, elevating clot risk. Second most common thrombophilia.
Loss-of-function variants reduce LDL-C and significantly lower cardiovascular disease risk.

interventions.action: Still monitor lipids, but likely favorable profile

drug_interactions.effect: May not need if naturally low LDL

snp_knowledge.description: Associated with elevated Lp(a), an independent cardiovascular risk factor not modifiable by lifestyle.

supplements.name: Niacin
supplements.rationale: Only supplement shown to lower Lp(a)

interventions.action: Aggressive apoB management even more critical
Test Lp(a) once - it's stable over lifetime

snp_knowledge.condition: 9p21 Risk Locus - Coronary artery disease risk
snp_knowledge.description: Strongest common genetic risk factor for CAD. Mechanism involves cell cycle regulation.
Strongest genetic risk factor for T2D. Impairs insulin secretion and incretin signaling.
Strongest obesity risk gene. Affects appetite regulation and energy expenditure. Each A allele +3kg average.

interventions.action: Regular exercise can overcome genetic predisposition
Track food intake - higher vigilance needed

snp_knowledge.description: The Ala variant improves insulin sensitivity and reduces T2D risk. More efficient fat storage.

interventions.action: Can tolerate higher carbs than average - still moderate

snp_knowledge.description: Associated with lower adiponectin levels, reducing insulin sensitivity and increasing inflammation.
Higher baseline IL-6 levels. Linked to cardiovascular disease and age-related decline.
Higher TNF-alpha production. Associated with increased inflammation and autoimmune risk.

snp_knowledge.condition: CRP genetic variant - Elevated baseline CRP
snp_knowledge.description: Genetically elevated CRP levels. Important to distinguish from lifestyle-driven inflammation.

interventions.action: Use genetic context when interpreting hsCRP results

snp_knowledge.description: Higher IL-1B production associated with chronic inflammation and periodontal disease risk.

interventions.action: Extra attention to oral health - higher periodontal risk

snp_knowledge.description: Slower caffeine metabolism. Higher cardiovascular risk with excess caffeine, sleep disruption.
Non-functional CYP2D6 allele. Affects metabolism of 25% of drugs including codeine, tamoxifen, antidepressants.

interventions.action: Inform physicians of CYP2D6 status for drug selection

snp_knowledge.description: Affects metabolism of 50% of drugs. Important for statins, immunosuppressants, many medications.
Absent GSTM1 enzyme reduces Phase II detoxification. Higher oxidative stress, cancer risk from carcinogens.

interventions.action: Cruciferous vegetables daily for other GST induction

snp_knowledge.description: Reduced GSTP1 activity affects detoxification of certain carcinogens and drugs.

snp_knowledge.condition: VDR FokI - Reduced vitamin D receptor function
snp_knowledge.description: Less efficient vitamin D receptor. May need higher vitamin D levels for same biological effect.

snp_knowledge.condition: GC - Lower vitamin D binding protein
snp_knowledge.description: Lower circulating 25(OH)D due to reduced binding protein. Higher supplementation needed.
Impaired conversion of beta-carotene to vitamin A. Need preformed vitamin A (retinol).

avoid.item: Relying on beta-carotene for vitamin A

interventions.action: Include liver, egg yolks, dairy for preformed vitamin A

snp_knowledge.description: Impaired vitamin C absorption and tissue accumulation. Higher intake may be needed.

interventions.action: High vitamin C foods throughout day: citrus, peppers, berries

snp_knowledge.description: G allele associated with exceptional longevity. Found in centenarians. Stress resistance gene.
Affects telomerase activity and telomere length. Complex effects - associated with both longevity and cancer risk.

interventions.action: Stress reduction - chronic stress shortens telomeres

snp_knowledge.description: Reduced CETP activity leads to higher HDL and larger HDL particles. Associated with longevity.
Associated with being a night owl, delayed sleep phase, and possible metabolic effects.

interventions.action: Morning bright light exposure to advance rhythm
Consistent sleep schedule even on weekends

snp_knowledge.description: Associated with being a morning person (early chronotype). Natural early risers.

interventions.action: Align schedule with natural early chronotype

snp_knowledge.description: Affects sleep duration needs. Some variants require more sleep for optimal cognitive function.

interventions.action: Prioritize adequate sleep duration for your genetics

snp_knowledge.description: XX genotype (TT) lacks alpha-actinin-3, shifting toward slow-twitch fibers. Better endurance, less power.

interventions.action: XX: Endurance sports advantage. RR: Power/sprint advantage. RX: Mixed.

snp_knowledge.description: I allele favors endurance (more efficient oxygen use). D allele favors power/strength.

interventions.action: II: Focus on endurance. DD: Power training advantage. ID: Balanced.

drug_interactions.effect: DD genotype may have enhanced response

snp_knowledge.description: Affects mitochondrial biogenesis and aerobic capacity. Key for endurance performance.

interventions.action: Zone 2 training particularly important for mitochondrial density

snp_knowledge.description: Reduced NO production affects blood vessel dilation and cardiovascular health.
TP53 is the most important tumor suppressor. Variant affects apoptosis efficiency.
G/G indicates likely lactose intolerance. A allele maintains lactase into adulthood.

interventions.action: Lactose-free options, hard cheeses, or plant milks

snp_knowledge.description: Major genetic risk factor for celiac disease. Not deterministic but required for celiac.

interventions.action: Screen for celiac if GI symptoms or family history

snp_knowledge.description: A1 allele associated with 30-40% fewer D2 receptors. Affects reward, addiction vulnerability.
G allele associated with higher empathy and social bonding. A allele may need more intentional social connection.
Short allele associated with increased stress sensitivity and depression risk in adverse environments.
Affects opioid receptor binding. G allele may have reduced pain relief from opioids, higher doses needed.

interventions.action: Inform physicians - may affect pain medication response

drug_interactions.effect: May need higher doses for pain relief

snp_knowledge.description: C allele significantly increases risk of statin-induced myopathy, especially with simvastatin.

interventions.action: Consider alternative statins: pravastatin, rosuvastatin lower risk

snp_knowledge.description: Ultra-rapid metabolism of CYP2C19 substrates. Clopidogrel more effective but some drugs less effective.

interventions.action: Inform physicians for drug selection and dosing

snp_knowledge.description: T allele requires lower warfarin doses. Critical for safe anticoagulation dosing.

avoid.item: Variable vitamin K intake on warfarin

interventions.action: Use FDA warfarin dosing guidelines incorporating genetics

drug_interactions.effect: Requires lower doses - TT genotype ~50% reduction

snp_knowledge.description: Reduced bilirubin conjugation causing benign elevated bilirubin (Gilbert's). Protective antioxidant effect.

interventions.action: Mild jaundice during fasting/illness is normal

snp_knowledge.description: Slower Phase II acetylation. Affects isoniazid, caffeine, and aromatic amine metabolism.
Reduced glutathione S-transferase Pi activity affecting detoxification of certain chemicals.
Affects conversion of plant omega-3s (ALA) to EPA/DHA. T allele = poor converter, needs preformed EPA/DHA.

interventions.action: Direct EPA/DHA sources: fatty fish, fish oil, algae

snp_knowledge.description: Common obesity risk variant. Affects satiety signaling and food reward.

interventions.action: High protein for satiety, structured meals

snp_knowledge.description: Additional FTO obesity risk variant. Affects energy balance and body composition.

interventions.action: Physical activity can overcome FTO effects

snp_knowledge.description: Affects cholesterol and lipid metabolism. Associated with weight gain susceptibility.
